headline: Micron, Anthropic sign AI infrastructure supply agreement
summary: Micron Technology said on Monday it has signed an agreement with Anthropic that includes supply ‌of memory and storage products, and a strategic investment ‌in the IPO-bound AI company's latest funding round.  AI developers are racing to secure ​critical components for increasingly expensive data-center buildouts, while memory makers look to tap into the soaring demand for high-bandwidth memory and storage used in training and running advanced AI models.  "Our compute strategy depends ‌on getting every layer ⁠of the stack right, and memory and storage are central to how efficiently we can train ⁠and serve Claude," said Tom Brown, Anthropic's co-founder and chief compute officer.

content.title: Micron, Anthropic sign AI infrastructure supply agreement
content.summary: Micron Technology said on Monday it has signed an agreement with Anthropic that includes supply ‌of memory and storage products, and a strategic investment ‌in the IPO-bound AI company's latest funding round.  AI developers are racing to secure ​critical components for increasingly expensive data-center buildouts, while memory makers look to tap into the soaring demand for high-bandwidth memory and storage used in training and running advanced AI models.  "Our compute strategy depends ‌on getting every layer ⁠of the stack right, and memory and storage are central to how efficiently we can train ⁠and serve Claude," said Tom Brown, Anthropic's co-founder and chief compute officer.

headline: JPMorgan Says Be an ‘Aggressive Buyer’ of This Beaten-Down AI Stock
summary: Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) posted record revenue of $22.19 billion on June 3, 2026, beat consensus on the top and bottom line, raised guidance, and lost roughly a fifth of its market value over the following week. The stock went from $495 at the filing to $385.73 one day later. The broader chip complex dropped alongside it. ... JPMorgan Says Be an ‘Aggressive Buyer’ of This Beaten-Down AI Stock

headline: Forget Nvidia: Four Billionaires Who Rarely Agree on Anything Hold the Same Overlooked Chip Stock
summary: Four billionaire portfolio managers who almost never end up on the same side of a trade quietly converged on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM). Chase Coleman’s Tiger Global added 49.38% to its Taiwan Semiconductor position. That lifted it to the fund’s fourth-largest holding at $1.04 billion. David Tepper boosted his stake alongside other chip names. Stanley ... Forget Nvidia: Four Billionaires Who Rarely Agree on Anything Hold the Same Overlooked Chip Stock
